display_description: A developer scaffold + reference standard for building multi-model OpenVINO pipeline demos (Intel AIPC only)
detail_describe: A developer-facing direction + reference standard (NOT a ready-made pipeline). Grounded in the openvino_notebooks repo, it resolves one or more notebooks (or a free-form goal) into a multi-model pipeline skeleton: discover stages, suggest per-role device (NPU/GPU/CPU) and precision (INT4/INT8 via NNCF), benchmark end-to-end + per-stage, and provide a client+server template to deploy the demo as a local service. The bundled scripts are reference implementations, not a mandatory path — model conversion and inference follow the chosen notebook's own code. It supplies shared conventions ([SKILL_RESULT] contract, lifecycle, directory layout); developers build their own pipeline demo on top. No hardcoded model IDs or Python/OpenVINO version pin. Unwired pipeline families return HTTP 501 instead of faking output. Runs locally, offline / China-mirror aware.
name: openvino-pipeline-optimization
use_cases: Scaffold a multi-model pipeline demo (code + reference standard) for developers
Discover and compose stages from notebook(s); assign device and precision
Benchmark end-to-end + per-stage and locate the bottleneck
Deploy the pipeline demo as a local service (client + server)
author: Intel AIPC Skills
